title: DTU RAVEN Achieves BlueDot Status and Prepares for SUAS Competition
excerpt: DTU RAVEN, a student-driven robotics team, has been officially recognized as a BlueDot project and is gearing up for the SUAS competition.
content:
DTU RAVEN (Robotics, Autonomous Vehicle Engineering & Navigation) is a student-driven initiative at the Technical University of Denmark (DTU) focused on developing cutting-edge autonomous systems and unmanned technologies. Our mission is to bridge the gap between academic theory and practical application, equipping students with hands-on experience in robotics, artificial intelligence, and autonomous navigation.

We are proud to announce that our organization has been officially approved as a BlueDot project, further cementing our position at the frontier of student innovation. This recognition highlights our commitment to bridging the gap between academia and industry, empowering students to turn cutting-edge research into real-world solutions. With this milestone, we are more motivated than ever to push the boundaries of autonomous technology.

Our primary focus is the completion of our autonomous drone, along with its advanced software systems. This will enable us to compete in the 23rd annual SUAS competition hosted by RoboNation, where we will showcase the engineering excellence of Denmark's top university DTU - Technical University of Denmark on the international stage.

We are always looking for passionate students, collaborators, and supporters to join us on this exciting journey. If you have a passion for autonomous systems and wish to collaborate on innovative projects, we invite you to become part of DTU RAVEN. To express your interest or to learn more about our initiatives, please contact us at contact@dturaven.com.
